title: NOC 32109 – Other Technical Occupations in Therapy and Assessment | GTR Immigration
description: Other Technical Occupations in Therapy and Assessment under NOC 32109 support rehabilitation, speech therapy, audiology, ophthalmology, and physiotherapy services across Canada. With growing demand for rehabilitation care, aging population services, and community-based therapy programs, this TEER 2 occupation continues to offer stable employment opportunities. NOC 32109 qualifies under several economic immigration programs, making it a structured pathway toward Canada PR for eligible professionals.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3210 – Technical occupations in therapy and assessmentNOC Code32109

duties: Conduct hearing evaluations and electro-acoustic testing
Provide patient education and follow-up services
Coordinate with audiologists and healthcare professionals
Communicative Disorders and Speech-Language Pathology Assistants
Assist with speech and language assessments
Assist in ophthalmic procedures and surgeries
Apply therapeutic techniques including massage, electrotherapy, and hydrotherapy
Advise clients on posture and exercise
Carry out treatment programs under supervision
Use therapeutic modalities to rehabilitate patients
Assist patients in regaining functional independence
employment_requirements: Hearing instrument practitioners: Two- or three-year diploma plus supervised training; licensing required in most provinces
Communicative disorders assistants: Three- to four-year college program
Ophthalmic technicians/technologists: Hospital-based training or supervised clinical training
Physical rehabilitation therapists (Quebec only): Three-year college program plus permit from Ordre professionnel de la physiothérapie du Québec
Physiotherapy assistants: Two-year PTA or rehabilitation therapy assistant program
Occupational therapy assistants: Two-year OTA college program
Regulatory or professional associations may include:
Joint Commission on Allied Health Personnel in Ophthalmology
Internationally trained professionals must complete credential recognition and, where required, provincial registration.
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range from $32,800 to $65,200, depending on province, specialization, and experience.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32109 remains stable nationwide. Growth drivers include:
Expansion of hearing and vision care services
Growth in outpatient and community care models
Certified professionals with Canadian-recognized credentials may experience stronger employment opportunities.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 32109 are employed in:
This positive employment balance suggests moderate but stable demand, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, and Saskatchewan.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32109?
Growing demand for rehabilitation and therapy services
Employment across both public and private healthcare sectors
Access to universal healthcare and long-term settlement benefits
Rehabilitation and therapy services remain essential to Canada’s healthcare system.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 32109, this occupation may provide a structured immigration pathway to Canada.
